sous_titres.text: The British Navy did its best,
but I'm still alive, my friend.
You'll not get away with this, Merrick!
- Hand over your sailing papers. - What for?
Because there can only be one of you,
Oh, you'll look very pretty in this uniform, Merrick,
but you'll not pass for an officer in Her Majesty's Navy.
You wait until my father comes home! He'll take care of you.
Your father isn't coming home. He's lost at sea.
<i>He is not! He'll be home soon.
He owes my father so much money he'd be better off lost.
Papa. I thought you were never coming home.
My leg got tangled in a jib line and, uh,
I was knocked over the side.
Jens cut me free before I drowned.
You showed Hakon what you brought for him?
Something I always dreamed about when I was your age.
It's a commission from our ship.
<i>Hakon, you're gonna be the new ship's boy!
Now you're ready, aren't you, son?
<i>And with that leg of yours we'll need him here.
Well, why didn't I think about that?
I guess you'll have to stay home.
<i>Heard about your accident. It's too bad.
<i>But they don't pay a man who's laid up in his bunk,
<i>so I'm afraid I have to ask you for a--
It doesn't look like I'll be going back to sea.
Then I don't see how I can extend your loan any longer.
You have to get off-- tomorrow.
My children were born here. My mother died here.
I'm going to sea-- to be ship's boy on the <i>Flora.
It looks like you are going to sea.
This was given to me by my first captain.
He said it would always bring me home safe.
And it always has. It's yours.
Let go gaskets on fore mainsail!
Hakon Hakonsen, sir. The new ship's boy.
There is no room for children on board this ship.
or I'll put you off in London.
No, sir! You can't! I have to sail for two years.
I can do anything I like on board this ship.
And never talk back to a captain. Never.
Nothing to it, kid, once you get the hang of it.
- I'll never learn how to do that. - I'll teach you right now.
I'm supposed to speak to the bosun.
Hakon Hakonsen, sir, the new ship's boy.
- Come for your orders, have you? - Yes, sir.
Did you see Jens tying a knot to that piece of rigging...
- Go! Untie it at once! - Untie it, sir?
<i>Hurry up, boy! Or are you trying to get us all killed?
<i>Wait! We can't send him up without a blindfold.
But Jens didn't have a blindfold.
You don't need a blindfold when you've been doing this...
It's breaking! Hurry! Hoist him up!
Right, right! Get him up there.
<i>That's it. Get the blindfold on his face.
One hand for the ship, and one hand for yourself--
Aw, just like you, Jens, to ruin the fun.
I remember when the trick was played on you, bosun.
<i>But your pants were too wet to fall down.
You'll find out everything you need to know.
A knot like this could cost a man his life.
Hakon, captain wants to see you.
Every man must be fit, or nobody can be safe.
Captain, please don't put me off. My family's depending on me.
<i>Have you ever seen one of these?
No, sir. It's called a photograph. Look at it.
<i>That's your father and I. I want you to have it.
I felt the same way when I was your age.
Get off me, you stinking bed bucket!
Anything of value, you better keep a close watch on around here.
I got my little brother with me. <i>Oh!
Looks like your brother could do with a little learning, hmm?
keep a close watch on your, uh, valuables.
Here you are, boys. Oh, yeah! Yeah!
Bottoms up! Bottoms up! Bottoms up!
Guys? You can't fool a real sailor.
<i>The voyage ahead takes us to Sydney,
That, as you all know, is pirate territory.
<i>The company has signed on a first mate...
<i>Is one of the most respected officers...
I'm pleased to have you and your bosun join us.
You are a fine body of seamen,
and I have no doubt that you will...
<i>Bring our ship to its destination as fast as nature allows.
Some of you may be concerned about pirates.
<i>Her majesty's Navy has dispatched these evil men...
- That is all. - Stand by, fore and aft!
Hey, ship's boy! Don't stand there sleeping.
You ought to be more careful with the property of the royal Navy, mister.
But we'll keep this to ourselves...
Every ship has her own secrets.
And every true sailor knows when to keep his mouth shut...
For the sake of his ship...
<i>Give a bit more. Go on. Go on.
spit it out. What's on your mind?
If a mate knows something about another mate,
or-or an officer-- kind of a secret--
if he knows that someone's got something on board...
That maybe they shouldn't, should he report him or not?
Has someone smuggled some rum on board?
I just want to be a true sailor, sir.
<i>Now for that secret of yours, sleep on it.
And in the morning, if you feel you should,
come and tell me about it, hmm?
Ay, yi, yi, yi, yi, yi, yi.
I have to talk to him.
So, how is the captain today?
Mm. Most likely, but no one else is sick.
I haven't seen ship's fever attack only one man, have you?
<i>We, therefore, commit his body to the deep,
♪ he maketh me down to lie ♪
Now there could be plenty of explanations for this cargo.
- Like what? - To use against pirates.
Then why does it say glass...
Captain Howell is a British naval officer, Hakon.
He is here to protect us.
It's not our place to question how he does it. Come on.
Sydney's the kind of harbor that leaves a Mark on a sailor.
Did on me. <i>Rig those cargo blocks! Go on!
I have an announcement from the captain.
We'll have to make up the lost time...
All shore leave here in Sydney is canceled.
Mr. Thatcher, we've been at sea...
must I remind you that this ship is under the command...
You didn't say anything about taking on extra crew, Mr. Howell.
We need these men to protect us in pirate waters.
Look, relax! I'm not gonna hurt you.
You've seen one before, haven't you?
what are you doing down here?
Going to Calcutta, same as you.
Why? <i>To get away from some busybodies...
Who want to put me in an orphanage, all right?
Why don't you stay with your parents?
They're dead, if you must know.
Why do you think I'm an orphan?
I have an uncle in Calcutta. That's why I'm going there.
You can have it if you keep quiet.
I can't read very well. I'll teach you.
You don't have to. I'll keep quiet.
My mother said it was the most beautiful place on earth.
But it's only in a book. It doesn't really exist.
- She's my friend. - A stowaway, huh?
She's an orphan. She has to get to her uncle in Calcutta.
<i>It's too late to tell you two how foolish this is--
Do you know what the penalty is for not reporting a stowaway?
I thought Hakon had smuggled a dog on board.
<i>you stick to your bunk at night.
He won't say anything, will he?
No. I trust him with my life.
There is a thief among us.
I want that man to stand forward and take his punishment.
One of you has been concealing her...
Ow! Prepare to keelhaul the prisoner.
Take her below. Clap her in irons.
You know the penalty for hiding a stowaway.
Do your duty or I shall do mine!
Go on! Cut the yard free! - <i>Cut the yard free! Go on!
You go! I'll hold the line!
We're not leaving without the kids!
Come on! Hurry up! She's sinking!
Merrick. He'll be back for his treasure one day.
Hakon? Hakon, Hakon, I knew you were alive.<i>
- This is no time for a reunion. - Wait!
What are you gonna do, kid? Take on the whole village by yourself?
They're peaceful, friendly. They saved our lives.
They plucked us out of the ocean,
and we've been traveling from island to island with them, trying to find you.
But Mary was fighting with them. Those boys.
They claim me as their sister, and now they expect me to clean up after them.
She fights with them every night.
I almost gave up hope of ever finding you again,
I, um, better go tell the chief we're leaving.
I thought I'd never see you again.
I thought I'd never see anybody again.
Merrick will be back for his treasure one day.
You step on that vine, and you'll be swinging from the top of that tree.
He's really put together quite a place here.
A real Xanadu, fit for a king.
But it wasn't quite like home.
I can't believe you ever got out of there alive the first time.
- Poor Merrick. - <i>Yes, he's really in for trouble.
We have to get out of sight.
- Don't any of them work? - I don't know!
<i>I don't believe it! Get out of there!
I want whoever done this deed. I want my treasure!
I want it found. I want every rock,
every hole, every tree on this island searched.
<i>You hear me? You hear me! <i>Aye, captain, right away.
Go back to the ship. Get the rest of the men. We spend the night here.
What about the prisoners? Bring a couple of 'em with ya.
That means our other mates probably are too.
Come on. I've got an idea.
This is so stupid. I'm the one who should go.
<i>Hakon knows the island best, and, Jens, you know everything else.
I sneaked on board your ship when you were standing guard.
Have you ever used a gun before?
I think I can handle myself.
Now, get the treasure safe aboard.
But wait for our signal, then carry out the plan.
But if it does, you sail on without us.
Take my share of the treasure to my family.
What are you looking at? Thought I heard something.
You've got to help me take over the ship.
You got a knife? The ropes.
There are only two guards left on board.
<i>It's that blasted boy and his friend to break out these fools!
- Don't let them escape! - They won't escape! Come on!
Go on. Get him loose. I'll be right behind you.
Oh! Oh! Cross over! I'm waiting for Jens!
- You gone crazy, lad? - Come on! Hurry up!
Stay there! I'll be right back!
Don't just stand there! Get after them! How?
Get a log! Make a bridge! Get after them! And get that brat!
The hollow tree! We made it!
You wasn't thinkin' of leavin', was ya?
Not without saying good-bye to your old mate? No.
You weren't thinkin' of betrayin' me, were you, Hakon?
<i>No. Not me what was so kind to ya.
I thought we was mates. I thought we was more than mates.
Now you listen here to me, boy.
I'm gonna count to three, and then you're gonna tell me where my treasure is.
'Cause if you don't, I'm gonna blow your head off.
Come back here, you little runt. I'll flay you alive!
Come on, you idiots! After them!
Get ready to sail! Hoist the mainsail!
Hoist the mainsail! Come on! Hurry up!
Keep her steady. I've got him in my sights.
<i>Come on! We're ready to sail! Come on!
Come back here, you-- come back here!
After them! Come on, you fools!
What are you waiting for? I'll get you, you--
They told us you were dead.
This is Mary. She'll be staying with us for a while.
Listen, Hakon, I'm afraid that none of us are able to stay here any longer.
It was sold this morning, papa.
Now, this is a long story. Come.
